content:
{
  "title": "Function Sketch Prompting: Structured Intermediate Guidance for Code Generation with LLMs",
  "problem": "Current prompt engineering strategies for LLM-based code generation often fail to scaffold the reasoning required for complex or multi-step programming tasks. There is a need for prompting methods that help LLMs plan code structure explicitly before generation.",
  "motivation": "As code generation tasks grow in complexity, LLMs struggle with multi-function or modular problems, yielding code that is brittle, incomplete, or poorly structured. Improving the ability of LLMs to plan and organize code before generation would unlock higher reliability and enable LLMs to address more challenging programming tasks. Recent advances in intermediate step prompting in language tasks suggest similar techniques could benefit code generation.",
  "method": "We propose 'Function Sketch Prompting', in which the prompt first elicits from the LLM a structured 'sketch'—a high-level outline of function signatures, docstrings, and stepwise comments—before asking it to generate the full code. The system prompt instructs the model to first output this sketch, then fill in the code for each sketched component, enforcing an explicit planning step. This method is compared both as a single prompt and as an explicit multi-turn conversation. The approach is designed to guide decomposition and modularization, especially for tasks requiring multiple functions and complex logic.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval and MBPP, focusing on multi-step or multi-function problems. Baselines include standard zero-shot prompting and chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting adapted for code. Metrics are functional correctness (pass@k), modularity (function decomposition score), and code quality (e.g., cyclomatic complexity). Ablations will compare single-turn and iterative sketch prompting."
}
